```python
          Cons: May look into future, same conceptual issue as 'forward'
    
    Returns:
    --------
    pandas.DataFrame
        df_oer with two new columns: rater_asg_uic_pde and snr_rater_asg_uic_pde
        Rows where rater/snr_rater is missing or no matching snapshot found
        will have NaN in the corresponding UIC column.
    """
    
    # Validate snapshot_match_method
    valid_methods = ['backward', 'forward', 'nearest']
    if snapshot_match_method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"snapshot_match_method must be one of {valid_methods}, got '{snapshot_match_method}'")
    
    # Map method to merge_asof direction parameter
    direction_map = {
        'backward': 'backward',
        'forward': 'forward',
        'nearest': 'nearest'
    }
    merge_direction = direction_map[snapshot_match_method]
    
    # Make copies to avoid modifying originals
    df_oer = df_oer.copy()
    df_master = df_short_master_pde.copy()
    
    # Ensure date columns are datetime
    df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    df_master[snpsht_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_master[snpsht_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    
    # Remove rows with missing dates
    df_master_clean = df_master[
        df_master[snpsht_dt_col].notna() & 
        df_master[pid_col].notna() &
        df_master[asg_uic_pde_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    # Sort master by pid_pde and snpsht_dt for merge_asof
    df_master_clean = df_master_clean.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col])
    
    print(f"📊 Master snapshot data: {len(df_master):,} rows, {len(df_master_clean):,} with valid dates/UICs")
    print(f"📊 OER data: {len(df_oer):,} rows")
    print(f"📊 Snapshot matching method: '{snapshot_match_method}' ({merge_direction})")
    
    # === Add Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_rater = df_oer[
        df_oer[rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid rater and date: {len(df_oer_rater):,} ({len(df_oer_rater)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_rater) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_rater['_original_index'] = df_oer_rater.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof: need to match rater pid_pde and eval_thru_dt
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_rater_sorted = df_oer_rater.sort_values([rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only raters
        df_master_rater = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_rater[rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_rater = df_master_rater.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique raters in OER: {df_oer_rater[rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for raters: {len(df_master_rater):,}")
        
        # Manual lookup approach to avoid pandas merge_asof sorting issues
        # Group by rater and perform lookup for each group
        rater_uic_results = []
        
        for rater_id in df_oer_rater_sorted[rater_col].unique():
            # Get OERs for this rater
            oer_mask = df_oer_rater_sorted[rater_col] == rater_id
            oer_subset = df_oer_rater_sorted[oer_mask].copy()
            
            # Get snapshots for this rater
            master_mask = df_master_rater[pid_col] == rater_id
            master_subset = df_master_rater[master_mask].copy()
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this rater, leave as NaN
                for idx in oer_subset['_original_index']:
                    rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col).reset_index(drop=True)
            # Ensure dates are numpy datetime64 for np.searchsorted
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # For each OER date, find the matching snapshot
            for _, oer_row in oer_subset.iterrows():
                # Convert to numpy datetime64 for consistent comparison
                oer_date = np.datetime64(pd.to_datetime(oer_row[eval_thru_dt_col]))
                orig_idx = oer_row['_original_index']
                
                if merge_direction == 'backward':
                    # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='right') - 1
                    if idx >= 0:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                    # Find next snapshot >= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='left')
                    if idx < len(master_uics):
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                    # Find closest snapshot
                    if len(master_dates) == 0:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                    else:
                        # Calculate absolute time differences
                        distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                        idx = np.argmin(distances)
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results = pd.DataFrame(rater_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results['_original_index'], rater_uic_col] = df_results[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_rater)*100:.1f}% of valid raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # === Add Senior Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up senior rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize senior rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col] = None
    df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col] = df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].astype('object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid senior rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_snr = df_oer[
        df_oer[snr_rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid senior rater and date: {len(df_oer_snr):,} ({len(df_oer_snr)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_snr) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_snr['_original_index'] = df_oer_snr.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_snr_sorted = df_oer_snr.sort_values([snr_rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only senior raters
        df_master_snr = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_snr = df_master_snr.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique senior raters in OER: {df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for senior raters: {len(df_master_snr):,}")
        
        # Manual lookup approach to avoid pandas merge_asof sorting issues
        # Group by senior rater and perform lookup for each group
        snr_uic_results = []
        
        for snr_id in df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col].unique():
            # Get OERs for this senior rater
            oer_mask = df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col] == snr_id
            oer_subset = df_oer_snr_sorted[oer_mask].copy()
            
            # Get snapshots for this senior rater
            master_mask = df_master_snr[pid_col] == snr_id
            master_subset = df_master_snr[master_mask].copy()
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this senior rater, leave as NaN
                for idx in oer_subset['_original_index']:
                    snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col).reset_index(drop=True)
            # Ensure dates are numpy datetime64 for np.searchsorted
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # For each OER date, find the matching snapshot
            for _, oer_row in oer_subset.iterrows():
                # Convert to numpy datetime64 for consistent comparison
                oer_date = np.datetime64(pd.to_datetime(oer_row[eval_thru_dt_col]))
                orig_idx = oer_row['_original_index']
                
                if merge_direction == 'backward':
                    # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='right') - 1
                    if idx >= 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                    # Find next snapshot >= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='left')
                    if idx < len(master_uics):
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                    # Find closest snapshot
                    if len(master_dates) == 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                    else:
                        # Calculate absolute time differences in days
                        distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                        idx = np.argmin(distances)
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results_snr = pd.DataFrame(snr_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results_snr['_original_index'], snr_rater_uic_col] = df_results_snr[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Senior rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_snr)*100:.1f}% of valid senior raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid senior raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # Summary
    print(f"\n✅ Summary:")
    print(f"  • Total OERs: {len(df_oer):,}")
    print(f"  • OERs with rater UIC: {df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"  • OERs with senior rater UIC: {df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    return df_oer


# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example OER data based on realistic organizational structure:
    # - Rated Officer: CPT, Commander of WJZEB0 (Bravo Company, 1-26 IN Battalion)
    # - Rater: Battalion Commander of WIZEAA (1-26 IN Battalion), assigned to WJZET0 (Battalion HHC)
    # - Senior Rater: Brigade Commander of WAB5FF (2nd Brigade, 101st ABN Division), assigned to WAB5AA (Brigade HHC)
    
    df_oer_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'rated_ofcr': ['CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002'],
        'eval_thru_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            '2017-05-12',  # OER ending in Q2 2017
            '2017-11-30',  # OER ending in Q4 2017
            '2017-04-30',  # OER ending in Q1 2017
            '2018-04-30'   # OER ending in Q2 2018
        ]),
        'rater': ['BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001'],  # Battalion Commander pid_pde
        'snr_rater': ['BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_002', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001']  # Brigade Commander pid_pde
    })
    
    # Example snapshot data (quarterly snapshots: 31 Dec, 31 Mar, 30 Jun, 30 Sep)
    # Shows where rated officers, raters, and senior raters were assigned at each snapshot date
    df_short_master_pde_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'pid_pde': [
            # Captain 001 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001',
            # Captain 002 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002',
            # Battalion Commander 001 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001',
            # Battalion Commander 002 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_002',
            # Brigade Commander 001 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001',
            # Brigade Commander 002 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_002'
        ],
        'snpsht_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            # CPT_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZEB0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZEB0
            # CPT_002 snapshots
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2018-03-31',  # Q1 2018 - WJZEB0
            # BN_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZET0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0
            # BN_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0 (took over)
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZET0
            # BDE_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WAB5AA
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WAB5AA
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WAB5AA
            # BDE_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30'   # Q3 2017 - WAB5AA (took over)
        ]),
        'asg_uic_pde': [
            # CPT_001 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # CPT_002 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # BN_CDR_001 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BN_CDR_002 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BDE_CDR_001 assignments (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA',
            # BDE_CDR_002 assignment (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA'
        ]
    })
    
    print("=" * 80)
    print("Example 1: Backward matching (default - recommended)")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_backward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='backward'
    )
    print("\nResult (backward):")
    print(result_backward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 2: Forward matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_forward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='forward'
    )
    print("\nResult (forward):")
    print(result_forward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 3: Nearest matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_nearest = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='nearest'
    )
    print("\nResult (nearest):")
    print(result_nearest[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])


```

```python
"""
Function to add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.

For each OER, looks up what UIC the rater and senior rater were assigned to
at the end of the evaluation period (eval_thru_dt).
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np


def add_rater_uic_to_oer(df_oer, df_short_master_pde,
                         eval_thru_dt_col='eval_thru_dt',
                         rater_col='rater',
                         snr_rater_col='snr_rater',
                         pid_col='pid_pde',
                         snpsht_dt_col='snpsht_dt',
                         asg_uic_pde_col='asg_uic_pde',
                         rater_uic_col='rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snr_rater_uic_col='snr_rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snapshot_match_method='backward'):
    """
    Add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.
    
    For each OER, finds a snapshot date based on the specified method
    and looks up the rater's and senior rater's asg_uic_pde at that time.
    
    Parameters:
    -----------
    df_oer : pandas.DataFrame
        OER dataframe with columns:
        - eval_thru_dt: End date of evaluation
        - rater: pid_pde of the rater
        - snr_rater: pid_pde of the senior rater
    df_short_master_pde : pandas.DataFrame
        Snapshot dataframe with columns:
        - pid_pde: Officer identifier
        - snpsht_dt: Snapshot date
        - asg_uic_pde: Assignment UIC at that snapshot
    eval_thru_dt_col : str, default 'eval_thru_dt'
        Column name for evaluation end date in df_oer
    rater_col : str, default 'rater'
        Column name for rater pid_pde in df_oer
    snr_rater_col : str, default 'snr_rater'
        Column name for senior rater pid_pde in df_oer
    pid_col : str, default 'pid_pde'
        Column name for officer ID in df_short_master_pde
    snpsht_dt_col : str, default 'snpsht_dt'
        Column name for snapshot date in df_short_master_pde
    asg_uic_pde_col : str, default 'asg_uic_pde'
        Column name for assignment UIC in df_short_master_pde
    rater_uic_col : str, default 'rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing rater's UIC
    snr_rater_uic_col : str, default 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing senior rater's UIC
    snapshot_match_method : str, default 'backward'
        Method for matching snapshot dates to eval_thru_dt:
        - 'backward': Uses most recent snapshot <= eval_thru_dt (recommended)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-03-31 snapshot
          Pros: Uses only information known at OER end date (temporally correct)
          Cons: Can be up to ~3 months stale with quarterly snapshots
        - 'forward': Uses next snapshot >= eval_thru_dt
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 snapshot
          Pros: Uses more recent information
          Cons: Uses data from after OER ended (looks into future)
        - 'nearest': Uses closest snapshot (either direction)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 (closer than 2017-03-31)
          Pros: Minimizes temporal distance
          Cons: May look into future, same conceptual issue as 'forward'
    
    Returns:
    --------
    pandas.DataFrame
        df_oer with two new columns: rater_asg_uic_pde and snr_rater_asg_uic_pde
        Rows where rater/snr_rater is missing or no matching snapshot found
        will have NaN in the corresponding UIC column.
    """
    
    # Validate snapshot_match_method
    valid_methods = ['backward', 'forward', 'nearest']
    if snapshot_match_method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"snapshot_match_method must be one of {valid_methods}, got '{snapshot_match_method}'")
    
    # Map method to merge_asof direction parameter
    direction_map = {
        'backward': 'backward',
        'forward': 'forward',
        'nearest': 'nearest'
    }
    merge_direction = direction_map[snapshot_match_method]
    
    # Make copies to avoid modifying originals
    df_oer = df_oer.copy()
    df_master = df_short_master_pde.copy()
    
    # Ensure date columns are datetime
    df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    df_master[snpsht_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_master[snpsht_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    
    # Remove rows with missing dates
    df_master_clean = df_master[
        df_master[snpsht_dt_col].notna() & 
        df_master[pid_col].notna() &
        df_master[asg_uic_pde_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    # Sort master by pid_pde and snpsht_dt for merge_asof
    df_master_clean = df_master_clean.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col])
    
    print(f"📊 Master snapshot data: {len(df_master):,} rows, {len(df_master_clean):,} with valid dates/UICs")
    print(f"📊 OER data: {len(df_oer):,} rows")
    print(f"📊 Snapshot matching method: '{snapshot_match_method}' ({merge_direction})")
    
    # === Add Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_rater = df_oer[
        df_oer[rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid rater and date: {len(df_oer_rater):,} ({len(df_oer_rater)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_rater) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_rater['_original_index'] = df_oer_rater.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof: need to match rater pid_pde and eval_thru_dt
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_rater_sorted = df_oer_rater.sort_values([rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only raters
        df_master_rater = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_rater[rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_rater = df_master_rater.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique raters in OER: {df_oer_rater[rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for raters: {len(df_master_rater):,}")
        
        # Manual lookup approach to avoid pandas merge_asof sorting issues
        # Group by rater and perform lookup for each group
        rater_uic_results = []
        
        for rater_id in df_oer_rater_sorted[rater_col].unique():
            # Get OERs for this rater
            oer_mask = df_oer_rater_sorted[rater_col] == rater_id
            oer_subset = df_oer_rater_sorted[oer_mask].copy()
            
            # Get snapshots for this rater
            master_mask = df_master_rater[pid_col] == rater_id
            master_subset = df_master_rater[master_mask].copy()
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this rater, leave as NaN
                for idx in oer_subset['_original_index']:
                    rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col).reset_index(drop=True)
            # Ensure dates are numpy datetime64 for np.searchsorted
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # For each OER date, find the matching snapshot
            for _, oer_row in oer_subset.iterrows():
                # Convert to numpy datetime64 for consistent comparison
                oer_date = np.datetime64(pd.to_datetime(oer_row[eval_thru_dt_col]))
                orig_idx = oer_row['_original_index']
                
                if merge_direction == 'backward':
                    # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='right') - 1
                    if idx >= 0:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                    # Find next snapshot >= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='left')
                    if idx < len(master_uics):
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                    # Find closest snapshot
                    if len(master_dates) == 0:
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                    else:
                        # Calculate absolute time differences
                        distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                        idx = np.argmin(distances)
                        rater_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results = pd.DataFrame(rater_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results['_original_index'], rater_uic_col] = df_results[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_rater)*100:.1f}% of valid raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # === Add Senior Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up senior rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize senior rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid senior rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_snr = df_oer[
        df_oer[snr_rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid senior rater and date: {len(df_oer_snr):,} ({len(df_oer_snr)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_snr) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_snr['_original_index'] = df_oer_snr.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_snr_sorted = df_oer_snr.sort_values([snr_rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only senior raters
        df_master_snr = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_snr = df_master_snr.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique senior raters in OER: {df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for senior raters: {len(df_master_snr):,}")
        
        # Manual lookup approach to avoid pandas merge_asof sorting issues
        # Group by senior rater and perform lookup for each group
        snr_uic_results = []
        
        for snr_id in df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col].unique():
            # Get OERs for this senior rater
            oer_mask = df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col] == snr_id
            oer_subset = df_oer_snr_sorted[oer_mask].copy()
            
            # Get snapshots for this senior rater
            master_mask = df_master_snr[pid_col] == snr_id
            master_subset = df_master_snr[master_mask].copy()
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this senior rater, leave as NaN
                for idx in oer_subset['_original_index']:
                    snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col).reset_index(drop=True)
            # Ensure dates are numpy datetime64 for np.searchsorted
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # For each OER date, find the matching snapshot
            for _, oer_row in oer_subset.iterrows():
                # Convert to numpy datetime64 for consistent comparison
                oer_date = np.datetime64(pd.to_datetime(oer_row[eval_thru_dt_col]))
                orig_idx = oer_row['_original_index']
                
                if merge_direction == 'backward':
                    # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='right') - 1
                    if idx >= 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                    # Find next snapshot >= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='left')
                    if idx < len(master_uics):
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                    # Find closest snapshot
                    if len(master_dates) == 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                    else:
                        # Calculate absolute time differences in days
                        distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                        idx = np.argmin(distances)
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results_snr = pd.DataFrame(snr_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results_snr['_original_index'], snr_rater_uic_col] = df_results_snr[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Senior rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_snr)*100:.1f}% of valid senior raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid senior raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # Summary
    print(f"\n✅ Summary:")
    print(f"  • Total OERs: {len(df_oer):,}")
    print(f"  • OERs with rater UIC: {df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"  • OERs with senior rater UIC: {df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    return df_oer


# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example OER data based on realistic organizational structure:
    # - Rated Officer: CPT, Commander of WJZEB0 (Bravo Company, 1-26 IN Battalion)
    # - Rater: Battalion Commander of WIZEAA (1-26 IN Battalion), assigned to WJZET0 (Battalion HHC)
    # - Senior Rater: Brigade Commander of WAB5FF (2nd Brigade, 101st ABN Division), assigned to WAB5AA (Brigade HHC)
    
    df_oer_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'rated_ofcr': ['CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002'],
        'eval_thru_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            '2017-05-12',  # OER ending in Q2 2017
            '2017-11-30',  # OER ending in Q4 2017
            '2017-04-30',  # OER ending in Q1 2017
            '2018-04-30'   # OER ending in Q2 2018
        ]),
        'rater': ['BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001'],  # Battalion Commander pid_pde
        'snr_rater': ['BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_002', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001']  # Brigade Commander pid_pde
    })
    
    # Example snapshot data (quarterly snapshots: 31 Dec, 31 Mar, 30 Jun, 30 Sep)
    # Shows where rated officers, raters, and senior raters were assigned at each snapshot date
    df_short_master_pde_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'pid_pde': [
            # Captain 001 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001',
            # Captain 002 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002',
            # Battalion Commander 001 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001',
            # Battalion Commander 002 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_002',
            # Brigade Commander 001 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001',
            # Brigade Commander 002 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_002'
        ],
        'snpsht_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            # CPT_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZEB0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZEB0
            # CPT_002 snapshots
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2018-03-31',  # Q1 2018 - WJZEB0
            # BN_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZET0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0
            # BN_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0 (took over)
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZET0
            # BDE_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WAB5AA
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WAB5AA
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WAB5AA
            # BDE_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30'   # Q3 2017 - WAB5AA (took over)
        ]),
        'asg_uic_pde': [
            # CPT_001 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # CPT_002 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # BN_CDR_001 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BN_CDR_002 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BDE_CDR_001 assignments (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA',
            # BDE_CDR_002 assignment (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA'
        ]
    })
    
    print("=" * 80)
    print("Example 1: Backward matching (default - recommended)")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_backward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='backward'
    )
    print("\nResult (backward):")
    print(result_backward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 2: Forward matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_forward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='forward'
    )
    print("\nResult (forward):")
    print(result_forward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 3: Nearest matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_nearest = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='nearest'
    )
    print("\nResult (nearest):")
    print(result_nearest[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])


```

Fixed the warnings. UIC columns were initialized with `np.nan` (which creates `float64`), but we assign string UIC codes.

**The fix:**
- Initialize the UIC columns with `pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')` to set the dtype to `object` from the start
- This allows string values to be assigned without dtype conflicts

Now when you assign UIC codes like `'WJZET0'` and `'WAB5AA'`, pandas won't warn because the columns are already set to `object` dtype, which can hold strings.

**What the warnings meant:**
- FutureWarning: pandas is warning that in future versions, assigning incompatible types (strings to float64 columns) will raise an error instead of automatically converting
- Fix: Initialize the columns with the correct dtype (`object`) from the start

Re-run your code; the warnings should be gone.

**User**

**Cursor**

Reviewing the code for efficiency issues. The nested loops with `iterrows()` may be slow on large datasets. Checking for optimizations:


Optimizing the code. `iterrows()` is slow; we can vectorize the lookups. Creating an optimized version:


```python
"""
Function to add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.

For each OER, looks up what UIC the rater and senior rater were assigned to
at the end of the evaluation period (eval_thru_dt).
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np


def add_rater_uic_to_oer(df_oer, df_short_master_pde,
                         eval_thru_dt_col='eval_thru_dt',
                         rater_col='rater',
                         snr_rater_col='snr_rater',
                         pid_col='pid_pde',
                         snpsht_dt_col='snpsht_dt',
                         asg_uic_pde_col='asg_uic_pde',
                         rater_uic_col='rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snr_rater_uic_col='snr_rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snapshot_match_method='backward'):
    """
    Add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.
    
    For each OER, finds a snapshot date based on the specified method
    and looks up the rater's and senior rater's asg_uic_pde at that time.
    
    Parameters:
    -----------
    df_oer : pandas.DataFrame
        OER dataframe with columns:
        - eval_thru_dt: End date of evaluation
        - rater: pid_pde of the rater
        - snr_rater: pid_pde of the senior rater
    df_short_master_pde : pandas.DataFrame
        Snapshot dataframe with columns:
        - pid_pde: Officer identifier
        - snpsht_dt: Snapshot date
        - asg_uic_pde: Assignment UIC at that snapshot
    eval_thru_dt_col : str, default 'eval_thru_dt'
        Column name for evaluation end date in df_oer
    rater_col : str, default 'rater'
        Column name for rater pid_pde in df_oer
    snr_rater_col : str, default 'snr_rater'
        Column name for senior rater pid_pde in df_oer
    pid_col : str, default 'pid_pde'
        Column name for officer ID in df_short_master_pde
    snpsht_dt_col : str, default 'snpsht_dt'
        Column name for snapshot date in df_short_master_pde
    asg_uic_pde_col : str, default 'asg_uic_pde'
        Column name for assignment UIC in df_short_master_pde
    rater_uic_col : str, default 'rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing rater's UIC
    snr_rater_uic_col : str, default 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing senior rater's UIC
    snapshot_match_method : str, default 'backward'
        Method for matching snapshot dates to eval_thru_dt:
        - 'backward': Uses most recent snapshot <= eval_thru_dt (recommended)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-03-31 snapshot
          Pros: Uses only information known at OER end date (temporally correct)
          Cons: Can be up to ~3 months stale with quarterly snapshots
        - 'forward': Uses next snapshot >= eval_thru_dt
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 snapshot
          Pros: Uses more recent information
          Cons: Uses data from after OER ended (looks into future)
        - 'nearest': Uses closest snapshot (either direction)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 (closer than 2017-03-31)
          Pros: Minimizes temporal distance
          Cons: May look into future, same conceptual issue as 'forward'
    
    Returns:
    --------
    pandas.DataFrame
        df_oer with two new columns: rater_asg_uic_pde and snr_rater_asg_uic_pde
        Rows where rater/snr_rater is missing or no matching snapshot found
        will have NaN in the corresponding UIC column.
    """
    
    # Validate snapshot_match_method
    valid_methods = ['backward', 'forward', 'nearest']
    if snapshot_match_method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"snapshot_match_method must be one of {valid_methods}, got '{snapshot_match_method}'")
    
    # Map method to merge_asof direction parameter
    direction_map = {
        'backward': 'backward',
        'forward': 'forward',
        'nearest': 'nearest'
    }
    merge_direction = direction_map[snapshot_match_method]
    
    # Make copies to avoid modifying originals
    df_oer = df_oer.copy()
    df_master = df_short_master_pde.copy()
    
    # Ensure date columns are datetime
    df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    df_master[snpsht_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_master[snpsht_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    
    # Remove rows with missing dates
    df_master_clean = df_master[
        df_master[snpsht_dt_col].notna() & 
        df_master[pid_col].notna() &
        df_master[asg_uic_pde_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    # Sort master by pid_pde and snpsht_dt for merge_asof
    df_master_clean = df_master_clean.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col])
    
    print(f"📊 Master snapshot data: {len(df_master):,} rows, {len(df_master_clean):,} with valid dates/UICs")
    print(f"📊 OER data: {len(df_oer):,} rows")
    print(f"📊 Snapshot matching method: '{snapshot_match_method}' ({merge_direction})")
    
    # === Add Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_rater = df_oer[
        df_oer[rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid rater and date: {len(df_oer_rater):,} ({len(df_oer_rater)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_rater) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_rater['_original_index'] = df_oer_rater.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof: need to match rater pid_pde and eval_thru_dt
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_rater_sorted = df_oer_rater.sort_values([rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only raters
        df_master_rater = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_rater[rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_rater = df_master_rater.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique raters in OER: {df_oer_rater[rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for raters: {len(df_master_rater):,}")
        
        # Optimized vectorized lookup approach
        # Group by rater and perform vectorized lookup for each group
        rater_uic_results = []
        
        # Group OER data by rater for efficient processing
        oer_groups = df_oer_rater_sorted.groupby(rater_col, sort=False)
        
        for rater_id, oer_group in oer_groups:
            # Get snapshots for this rater
            master_mask = df_master_rater[pid_col] == rater_id
            master_subset = df_master_rater[master_mask]
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this rater, leave as NaN
                rater_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan} 
                                          for idx in oer_group['_original_index'].values])
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date once
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col)
            # Convert to numpy arrays for vectorized operations
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # Vectorize OER dates
            oer_dates = pd.to_datetime(oer_group[eval_thru_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            orig_indices = oer_group['_original_index'].values
            
            # Vectorized lookup for all OERs for this rater
            if merge_direction == 'backward':
                # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='right') - 1
                # Clip negative indices to -1 (will be handled as NaN)
                uics = np.where(indices >= 0, master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                # Find next snapshot >= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='left')
                uics = np.where(indices < len(master_uics), master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                # Find closest snapshot for each OER
                uics = []
                for oer_date in oer_dates:
                    distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                    idx = np.argmin(distances)
                    uics.append(master_uics[idx])
                uics = np.array(uics)
            
            # Convert None to np.nan for consistency
            uics = np.where(uics == None, np.nan, uics)
            
            # Build results list
            rater_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: uic} 
                                      for idx, uic in zip(orig_indices, uics)])
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results = pd.DataFrame(rater_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results['_original_index'], rater_uic_col] = df_results[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_rater)*100:.1f}% of valid raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # === Add Senior Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up senior rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize senior rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid senior rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_snr = df_oer[
        df_oer[snr_rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid senior rater and date: {len(df_oer_snr):,} ({len(df_oer_snr)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_snr) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_snr['_original_index'] = df_oer_snr.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_snr_sorted = df_oer_snr.sort_values([snr_rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only senior raters
        df_master_snr = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_snr = df_master_snr.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique senior raters in OER: {df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for senior raters: {len(df_master_snr):,}")
        
        # Manual lookup approach to avoid pandas merge_asof sorting issues
        # Group by senior rater and perform lookup for each group
        snr_uic_results = []
        
        for snr_id in df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col].unique():
            # Get OERs for this senior rater
            oer_mask = df_oer_snr_sorted[snr_rater_col] == snr_id
            oer_subset = df_oer_snr_sorted[oer_mask].copy()
            
            # Get snapshots for this senior rater
            master_mask = df_master_snr[pid_col] == snr_id
            master_subset = df_master_snr[master_mask].copy()
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this senior rater, leave as NaN
                for idx in oer_subset['_original_index']:
                    snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col).reset_index(drop=True)
            # Ensure dates are numpy datetime64 for np.searchsorted
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # For each OER date, find the matching snapshot
            for _, oer_row in oer_subset.iterrows():
                # Convert to numpy datetime64 for consistent comparison
                oer_date = np.datetime64(pd.to_datetime(oer_row[eval_thru_dt_col]))
                orig_idx = oer_row['_original_index']
                
                if merge_direction == 'backward':
                    # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='right') - 1
                    if idx >= 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                    # Find next snapshot >= oer_date
                    idx = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_date, side='left')
                    if idx < len(master_uics):
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
                    else:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                        
                elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                    # Find closest snapshot
                    if len(master_dates) == 0:
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan})
                    else:
                        # Calculate absolute time differences in days
                        distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                        idx = np.argmin(distances)
                        snr_uic_results.append({'_original_index': orig_idx, asg_uic_pde_col: master_uics[idx]})
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results_snr = pd.DataFrame(snr_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results_snr['_original_index'], snr_rater_uic_col] = df_results_snr[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Senior rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_snr)*100:.1f}% of valid senior raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid senior raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # Summary
    print(f"\n✅ Summary:")
    print(f"  • Total OERs: {len(df_oer):,}")
    print(f"  • OERs with rater UIC: {df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"  • OERs with senior rater UIC: {df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    return df_oer


# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example OER data based on realistic organizational structure:
    # - Rated Officer: CPT, Commander of WJZEB0 (Bravo Company, 1-26 IN Battalion)
    # - Rater: Battalion Commander of WIZEAA (1-26 IN Battalion), assigned to WJZET0 (Battalion HHC)
    # - Senior Rater: Brigade Commander of WAB5FF (2nd Brigade, 101st ABN Division), assigned to WAB5AA (Brigade HHC)
    
    df_oer_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'rated_ofcr': ['CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002'],
        'eval_thru_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            '2017-05-12',  # OER ending in Q2 2017
            '2017-11-30',  # OER ending in Q4 2017
            '2017-04-30',  # OER ending in Q1 2017
            '2018-04-30'   # OER ending in Q2 2018
        ]),
        'rater': ['BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001'],  # Battalion Commander pid_pde
        'snr_rater': ['BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_002', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001']  # Brigade Commander pid_pde
    })
    display(df_oer_example)
    print("=" * 80)
    # Example snapshot data (quarterly snapshots: 31 Dec, 31 Mar, 30 Jun, 30 Sep)
    # Shows where rated officers, raters, and senior raters were assigned at each snapshot date
    df_short_master_pde_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'pid_pde': [
            # Captain 001 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001',
            # Captain 002 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002',
            # Battalion Commander 001 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001',
            # Battalion Commander 002 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_002',
            # Brigade Commander 001 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001',
            # Brigade Commander 002 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_002'
        ],
        'snpsht_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            # CPT_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZEB0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZEB0
            # CPT_002 snapshots
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2018-03-31',  # Q1 2018 - WJZEB0
            # BN_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZET0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0
            # BN_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0 (took over)
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZET0
            # BDE_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WAB5AA
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WAB5AA
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WAB5AA
            # BDE_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30'   # Q3 2017 - WAB5AA (took over)
        ]),
        'asg_uic_pde': [
            # CPT_001 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # CPT_002 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # BN_CDR_001 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BN_CDR_002 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BDE_CDR_001 assignments (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA',
            # BDE_CDR_002 assignment (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA'
        ]
    })
    display(df_oer_example)
    print("=" * 80)
    print("Example 1: Backward matching (default - recommended)")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_backward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='backward'
    )
    print("\nResult (backward):")
    print(result_backward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 2: Forward matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_forward = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='forward'
    )
    print("\nResult (forward):")
    print(result_forward[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("Example 3: Nearest matching")
    print("=" * 80)
    result_nearest = add_rater_uic_to_oer(
        df_oer_example, 
        df_short_master_pde_example,
        snapshot_match_method='nearest'
    )
    print("\nResult (nearest):")
    print(result_nearest[['rated_ofcr', 'eval_thru_dt', 'rater', 'snr_rater', 
                          'rater_asg_uic_pde', 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde']])


```

```python
"""
Function to add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.

For each OER, looks up what UIC the rater and senior rater were assigned to
at the end of the evaluation period (eval_thru_dt).
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np


def add_rater_uic_to_oer(df_oer, df_short_master_pde,
                         eval_thru_dt_col='eval_thru_dt',
                         rater_col='rater',
                         snr_rater_col='snr_rater',
                         pid_col='pid_pde',
                         snpsht_dt_col='snpsht_dt',
                         asg_uic_pde_col='asg_uic_pde',
                         rater_uic_col='rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snr_rater_uic_col='snr_rater_asg_uic_pde',
                         snapshot_match_method='backward'):
    """
    Add rater and senior rater assignment UICs to OER dataframe.
    
    For each OER, finds a snapshot date based on the specified method
    and looks up the rater's and senior rater's asg_uic_pde at that time.
    
    Parameters:
    -----------
    df_oer : pandas.DataFrame
        OER dataframe with columns:
        - eval_thru_dt: End date of evaluation
        - rater: pid_pde of the rater
        - snr_rater: pid_pde of the senior rater
    df_short_master_pde : pandas.DataFrame
        Snapshot dataframe with columns:
        - pid_pde: Officer identifier
        - snpsht_dt: Snapshot date
        - asg_uic_pde: Assignment UIC at that snapshot
    eval_thru_dt_col : str, default 'eval_thru_dt'
        Column name for evaluation end date in df_oer
    rater_col : str, default 'rater'
        Column name for rater pid_pde in df_oer
    snr_rater_col : str, default 'snr_rater'
        Column name for senior rater pid_pde in df_oer
    pid_col : str, default 'pid_pde'
        Column name for officer ID in df_short_master_pde
    snpsht_dt_col : str, default 'snpsht_dt'
        Column name for snapshot date in df_short_master_pde
    asg_uic_pde_col : str, default 'asg_uic_pde'
        Column name for assignment UIC in df_short_master_pde
    rater_uic_col : str, default 'rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing rater's UIC
    snr_rater_uic_col : str, default 'snr_rater_asg_uic_pde'
        Name for new column containing senior rater's UIC
    snapshot_match_method : str, default 'backward'
        Method for matching snapshot dates to eval_thru_dt:
        - 'backward': Uses most recent snapshot <= eval_thru_dt (recommended)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-03-31 snapshot
          Pros: Uses only information known at OER end date (temporally correct)
          Cons: Can be up to ~3 months stale with quarterly snapshots
        - 'forward': Uses next snapshot >= eval_thru_dt
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 snapshot
          Pros: Uses more recent information
          Cons: Uses data from after OER ended (looks into future)
        - 'nearest': Uses closest snapshot (either direction)
          Example: OER ends 2017-05-12 → uses 2017-06-30 (closer than 2017-03-31)
          Pros: Minimizes temporal distance
          Cons: May look into future, same conceptual issue as 'forward'
    
    Returns:
    --------
    pandas.DataFrame
        df_oer with two new columns: rater_asg_uic_pde and snr_rater_asg_uic_pde
        Rows where rater/snr_rater is missing or no matching snapshot found
        will have NaN in the corresponding UIC column.
    """
    
    # Validate snapshot_match_method
    valid_methods = ['backward', 'forward', 'nearest']
    if snapshot_match_method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"snapshot_match_method must be one of {valid_methods}, got '{snapshot_match_method}'")
    
    # Map method to merge_asof direction parameter
    direction_map = {
        'backward': 'backward',
        'forward': 'forward',
        'nearest': 'nearest'
    }
    merge_direction = direction_map[snapshot_match_method]
    
    # Make copies to avoid modifying originals
    df_oer = df_oer.copy()
    df_master = df_short_master_pde.copy()
    
    # Ensure date columns are datetime
    df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    df_master[snpsht_dt_col] = pd.to_datetime(df_master[snpsht_dt_col], errors='coerce')
    
    # Remove rows with missing dates
    df_master_clean = df_master[
        df_master[snpsht_dt_col].notna() & 
        df_master[pid_col].notna() &
        df_master[asg_uic_pde_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    # Sort master by pid_pde and snpsht_dt for merge_asof
    df_master_clean = df_master_clean.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col])
    
    print(f"📊 Master snapshot data: {len(df_master):,} rows, {len(df_master_clean):,} with valid dates/UICs")
    print(f"📊 OER data: {len(df_oer):,} rows")
    print(f"📊 Snapshot matching method: '{snapshot_match_method}' ({merge_direction})")
    
    # === Add Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_rater = df_oer[
        df_oer[rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid rater and date: {len(df_oer_rater):,} ({len(df_oer_rater)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_rater) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_rater['_original_index'] = df_oer_rater.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof: need to match rater pid_pde and eval_thru_dt
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_rater_sorted = df_oer_rater.sort_values([rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only raters
        df_master_rater = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_rater[rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_rater = df_master_rater.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique raters in OER: {df_oer_rater[rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for raters: {len(df_master_rater):,}")
        
        # Optimized vectorized lookup approach
        # Group by rater and perform vectorized lookup for each group
        rater_uic_results = []
        
        # Group OER data by rater for efficient processing
        oer_groups = df_oer_rater_sorted.groupby(rater_col, sort=False)
        
        for rater_id, oer_group in oer_groups:
            # Get snapshots for this rater
            master_mask = df_master_rater[pid_col] == rater_id
            master_subset = df_master_rater[master_mask]
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this rater, leave as NaN
                rater_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan} 
                                          for idx in oer_group['_original_index'].values])
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date once
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col)
            # Convert to numpy arrays for vectorized operations
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # Vectorize OER dates
            oer_dates = pd.to_datetime(oer_group[eval_thru_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            orig_indices = oer_group['_original_index'].values
            
            # Vectorized lookup for all OERs for this rater
            if merge_direction == 'backward':
                # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='right') - 1
                # Clip negative indices to -1 (will be handled as NaN)
                uics = np.where(indices >= 0, master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                # Find next snapshot >= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='left')
                uics = np.where(indices < len(master_uics), master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                # Find closest snapshot for each OER
                uics = []
                for oer_date in oer_dates:
                    distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                    idx = np.argmin(distances)
                    uics.append(master_uics[idx])
                uics = np.array(uics)
            
            # Convert None to np.nan for consistency
            uics = np.where(uics == None, np.nan, uics)
            
            # Build results list
            rater_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: uic} 
                                      for idx, uic in zip(orig_indices, uics)])
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results = pd.DataFrame(rater_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results['_original_index'], rater_uic_col] = df_results[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_rater)*100:.1f}% of valid raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # === Add Senior Rater UIC ===
    print(f"\n🔍 Looking up senior rater UICs...")
    
    # Initialize senior rater UIC column with object dtype to accept string UIC codes
    df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col] = pd.Series(index=df_oer.index, dtype='object')
    
    # Filter to OERs with valid senior rater and eval_thru_dt
    df_oer_snr = df_oer[
        df_oer[snr_rater_col].notna() & 
        df_oer[eval_thru_dt_col].notna()
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"  • OERs with valid senior rater and date: {len(df_oer_snr):,} ({len(df_oer_snr)/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    if len(df_oer_snr) > 0:
        # Save original index for merging back
        df_oer_snr['_original_index'] = df_oer_snr.index
        
        # Prepare for merge_asof
        # merge_asof requires the left DataFrame to be sorted by [by_column, date_column]
        # Reset index after sorting to ensure clean sequential indexing
        df_oer_snr_sorted = df_oer_snr.sort_values([snr_rater_col, eval_thru_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        # Filter master to only senior raters
        df_master_snr = df_master_clean[
            df_master_clean[pid_col].isin(df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].unique())
        ].copy()
        
        # Ensure master is also sorted properly for merge_asof
        df_master_snr = df_master_snr.sort_values([pid_col, snpsht_dt_col]).reset_index(drop=True)
        
        print(f"  • Unique senior raters in OER: {df_oer_snr[snr_rater_col].nunique():,}")
        print(f"  • Snapshot records for senior raters: {len(df_master_snr):,}")
        
        # Optimized vectorized lookup approach
        # Group by senior rater and perform vectorized lookup for each group
        snr_uic_results = []
        
        # Group OER data by senior rater for efficient processing
        oer_groups = df_oer_snr_sorted.groupby(snr_rater_col, sort=False)
        
        for snr_id, oer_group in oer_groups:
            # Get snapshots for this senior rater
            master_mask = df_master_snr[pid_col] == snr_id
            master_subset = df_master_snr[master_mask]
            
            if len(master_subset) == 0:
                # No snapshots for this senior rater, leave as NaN
                snr_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: np.nan} 
                                        for idx in oer_group['_original_index'].values])
                continue
            
            # Sort master by date once
            master_subset = master_subset.sort_values(snpsht_dt_col)
            # Convert to numpy arrays for vectorized operations
            master_dates = pd.to_datetime(master_subset[snpsht_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            master_uics = master_subset[asg_uic_pde_col].values
            
            # Vectorize OER dates
            oer_dates = pd.to_datetime(oer_group[eval_thru_dt_col]).values.astype('datetime64[ns]')
            orig_indices = oer_group['_original_index'].values
            
            # Vectorized lookup for all OERs for this senior rater
            if merge_direction == 'backward':
                # Find most recent snapshot <= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='right') - 1
                # Clip negative indices to -1 (will be handled as NaN)
                uics = np.where(indices >= 0, master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'forward':
                # Find next snapshot >= oer_date for each OER
                indices = np.searchsorted(master_dates, oer_dates, side='left')
                uics = np.where(indices < len(master_uics), master_uics[indices], None)
                
            elif merge_direction == 'nearest':
                # Find closest snapshot for each OER
                uics = []
                for oer_date in oer_dates:
                    distances = np.abs((master_dates - oer_date) / np.timedelta64(1, 'D'))
                    idx = np.argmin(distances)
                    uics.append(master_uics[idx])
                uics = np.array(uics)
            
            # Convert None to np.nan for consistency
            uics = np.where(uics == None, np.nan, uics)
            
            # Build results list
            snr_uic_results.extend([{'_original_index': idx, asg_uic_pde_col: uic} 
                                    for idx, uic in zip(orig_indices, uics)])
        
        # Convert results to DataFrame and map back
        df_results_snr = pd.DataFrame(snr_uic_results)
        df_oer.loc[df_results_snr['_original_index'], snr_rater_uic_col] = df_results_snr[asg_uic_pde_col].values
        
        # Count matches
        n_matched = df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()
        print(f"  • Senior rater UICs matched: {n_matched:,} ({n_matched/len(df_oer_snr)*100:.1f}% of valid senior raters)")
    else:
        print(f"  • No valid senior raters found, setting all to NaN")
    
    # Summary
    print(f"\n✅ Summary:")
    print(f"  • Total OERs: {len(df_oer):,}")
    print(f"  • OERs with rater UIC: {df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"  • OERs with senior rater UIC: {df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum():,} ({df_oer[snr_rater_uic_col].notna().sum()/len(df_oer)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    return df_oer


# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example OER data based on realistic organizational structure:
    # - Rated Officer: CPT, Commander of WJZEB0 (Bravo Company, 1-26 IN Battalion)
    # - Rater: Battalion Commander of WIZEAA (1-26 IN Battalion), assigned to WJZET0 (Battalion HHC)
    # - Senior Rater: Brigade Commander of WAB5FF (2nd Brigade, 101st ABN Division), assigned to WAB5AA (Brigade HHC)
    
    df_oer_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'rated_ofcr': ['CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002'],
        'eval_thru_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            '2017-05-12',  # OER ending in Q2 2017
            '2017-11-30',  # OER ending in Q4 2017
            '2017-04-30',  # OER ending in Q1 2017
            '2018-04-30'   # OER ending in Q2 2018
        ]),
        'rater': ['BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001'],  # Battalion Commander pid_pde
        'snr_rater': ['BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_002', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001']  # Brigade Commander pid_pde
    })
    display(df_oer_example)
    print("=" * 80)
    # Example snapshot data (quarterly snapshots: 31 Dec, 31 Mar, 30 Jun, 30 Sep)
    # Shows where rated officers, raters, and senior raters were assigned at each snapshot date
    df_short_master_pde_example = pd.DataFrame({
        'pid_pde': [
            # Captain 001 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001', 'CPT_001',
            # Captain 002 (assigned to WJZEB0 - Bravo Company)
            'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002', 'CPT_002',
            # Battalion Commander 001 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001', 'BN_CDR_001',
            # Battalion Commander 002 (assigned to WJZET0 - Battalion HHC)
            'BN_CDR_002', 'BN_CDR_002',
            # Brigade Commander 001 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001', 'BDE_CDR_001',
            # Brigade Commander 002 (assigned to WAB5AA - Brigade HHC)
            'BDE_CDR_002'
        ],
        'snpsht_dt': pd.to_datetime([
            # CPT_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZEB0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZEB0
            # CPT_002 snapshots
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZEB0
            '2018-03-31',  # Q1 2018 - WJZEB0
            # BN_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WJZET0
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WJZET0
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0
            # BN_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30',  # Q3 2017 - WJZET0 (took over)
            '2017-12-31',  # Q4 2017 - WJZET0
            # BDE_CDR_001 snapshots
            '2016-12-31',  # Q4 2016 - WAB5AA
            '2017-03-31',  # Q1 2017 - WAB5AA
            '2017-06-30',  # Q2 2017 - WAB5AA
            # BDE_CDR_002 snapshots
            '2017-09-30'   # Q3 2017 - WAB5AA (took over)
        ]),
        'asg_uic_pde': [
            # CPT_001 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # CPT_002 assignments (always at Bravo Company)
            'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0', 'WJZEB0',
            # BN_CDR_001 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BN_CDR_002 assignments (always at Battalion HHC)
            'WJZET0', 'WJZET0',
            # BDE_CDR_001 assignments (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA', 'WAB5AA',
            # BDE_CDR_002 assignment (always at Brigade HHC)
            'WAB5AA'
        ]
    })
    display(df_oer_example)
    print("=" * 80)
```
